# SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000 - QUIC Transport (RFC 9000)

## Purpose

Capture the transport requirements defined by RFC 9000 as canonical spec-trace clauses.

## Scope

This specification covers the reviewed RFC 9000 transport extraction outputs across all temporary batch ranges while collapsing duplicate batch coverage into one canonical RFC-level specification.

## Context

RFC 9000 is the largest QUIC source in this assembly. The temporary extraction batches overlap in places, especially around sections 6 through 10, so the assembly preserves section-level provenance while deduplicating only exact same-section duplicates.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2-0001 Give streams an ordered byte-stream abstraction
Streams in QUIC MUST provide applications with an ordered byte-stream abstraction.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §2 RFC9000-S2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2

Notes:
- The word "lightweight" is descriptive and not separately elevated.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2-0002 Permit streams to be created by sending data
Streams in QUIC MAY be created by sending data.

Streams in QUIC MAY last for the entire duration of a connection.

Streams in QUIC MAY be created by either endpoint.

Streams in QUIC MAY concurrently send data interleaved with other streams.

Streams in QUIC MAY be canceled.

QUIC MAY allow an arbitrary number of streams to operate concurrently.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0001 Constrain unidirectional stream direction
A unidirectional stream MUST carry data only from the initiator of the stream to its peer.

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0002 Constrain bidirectional stream direction
A bidirectional stream MUST allow data to be sent in both directions.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0003 Identify streams by stream ID
Each stream MUST be identified within a connection by a numeric value called the stream ID.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §2.1 RFC9000-S2.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0004 Bound stream IDs to 62 bits
A stream ID MUST be a 62-bit integer in the range 0 to 2^62-1.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §2.1 RFC9000-S2.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2.1

Notes:
- The ledger renders the numeric expression without the exponent marker; the RFC text intends the 62-bit range.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0007 Forbid stream ID reuse
A QUIC endpoint MUST NOT reuse a stream ID within a connection.

The least significant bit of a stream ID MUST identify the initiator of the stream.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0009 Use even IDs for client-initiated streams
Client-initiated streams MUST have even-numbered stream IDs with the least significant bit set to 0.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

Server-initiated streams MUST have odd-numbered stream IDs with the least significant bit set to 1.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0011 Use the second low bit to distinguish stream direction
The second least significant bit of a stream ID MUST distinguish bidirectional streams from unidirectional streams, with 0 indicating bidirectional and 1 indicating unidirectional.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0012 Start each stream space at its minimum value
The stream space for each type MUST begin at its minimum value.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0013 Increase stream IDs numerically
Successive streams of each type MUST be created with numerically increasing stream IDs.

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P1-0014 Open lower-numbered streams when a stream ID is used out of order
Using a stream ID out of order MUST cause all lower-numbered streams of that type to be opened.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0001 Encapsulate application data in STREAM frames
STREAM frames MUST encapsulate data sent by an application.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0002 Use stream and offset fields to order data
An endpoint MUST use the Stream ID and Offset fields in STREAM frames to place data in order.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0003 Deliver stream data as an ordered byte stream
Endpoints MUST be able to deliver stream data to an application as an ordered byte stream.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0004 Buffer out-of-order stream data until it can be delivered in order
To deliver an ordered byte stream, an endpoint MUST buffer any out-of-order data it receives up to the advertised flow control limit.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0005 Allow optional out-of-order delivery to applications
Implementations MAY offer the ability to deliver stream data out of order to a receiving application.

An endpoint MAY discard data that has already been received.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0007 Keep retransmitted stream data stable at the same offset
The data at a given offset MUST NOT change if it is sent multiple times.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0008 Permit treating conflicting retransmissions as protocol violations
An endpoint MAY treat receipt of different data at the same offset within a stream as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0009 Expose streams as ordered byte streams only
Streams MUST be an ordered byte-stream abstraction with no other structure visible to QUIC.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P2-0010 Respect peer flow control limits before sending
An endpoint MUST NOT send data on any stream without ensuring that it is within the flow control limits set by its peer.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P3-0001 Avoid a built-in prioritization exchange
QUIC MUST NOT provide a mechanism for exchanging prioritization information.

A QUIC implementation SHOULD provide ways in which an application can indicate the relative priority of streams.

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S2P4-0008 Allow stream state-change notifications
An application protocol MAY request to be informed of stream state changes, including when the peer has opened or reset a stream, when a peer aborts reading on a stream, when new data is available, and when data can or cannot be written to the stream due to flow control.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §2.4 RFC9000-S2.4-B10-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-2.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3-0001 Choose the correct state machine for unidirectional streams
Unidirectional streams MUST use either the sending or receiving state machine, depending on the stream type and endpoint role.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3 RFC9000-S3-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3-0002 Use both state machines for bidirectional streams
Bidirectional streams MUST use both state machines at both endpoints.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3 RFC9000-S3-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3-0003 Allow alternate stream state machines with consistent behavior
An implementation MAY define a different stream state machine as long as its behavior is consistent with an implementation that implements these states.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3 RFC9000-S3-B4-P3-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0001 Treat Ready as a newly created sendable stream
The Ready state MUST represent a newly created stream that is able to accept data from the application.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B5-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0002 Permit buffering in Ready
An implementation MAY buffer stream data in the Ready state in preparation for sending.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B5-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0003 Enter Send state on first outbound stream frame
Sending the first STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame MUST cause a sending part of a stream to enter the Send state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0003.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsReceiveOnlyPeerUnidirectionalStreamsWithoutEnteringSendState
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0003.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_EntersSendStateOnFirstBlockedOutboundFrame

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0004 Allow deferred stream ID allocation
An implementation MAY defer allocating a stream ID to a stream until it sends the first STREAM frame and enters the Send state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B6-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0005 Start peer-initiated bidirectional senders in Ready
The sending part of a bidirectional stream initiated by a peer MUST start in the Ready state when the receiving part is created.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B7-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0005.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_DoesNotStartPeerInitiatedUnidirectionalSendersInReady

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0006 Transmit and retransmit in Send
In the Send state, an endpoint MUST transmit and retransmit as necessary stream data in STREAM frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B8-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0006.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsPeerInitiatedUnidirectionalStreamsWithoutASendPart

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0007 Respect peer flow control while sending
An endpoint in the Send state MUST respect the flow control limits set by its peer and continue to accept and process MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B8-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0007.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_AcceptsMaxStreamDataAfterEnteringSendState

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0008 Signal Send blocking with STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED
An endpoint in the Send state MUST generate STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames if it is blocked from sending by stream flow control limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B8-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0008.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_GeneratesStreamDataBlockedWhenBlockedByFlowControl

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0009 Enter Data Sent after sending FIN
After the application indicates that all stream data has been sent and a STREAM frame containing the FIN bit is sent, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Data Sent state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B9-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0009.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_EntersDataSentAfterFin
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0009.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_EntersDataSentOnZeroLengthFin
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0009.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_LeavesStreamInSendStateWithoutFin

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0010 Restrict Data Sent retransmissions
From the Data Sent state, the endpoint MUST retransmit stream data only as necessary.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B9-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0010.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsBytesBeyondTheKnownFinalSizeAfterDataSent

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0011 Stop flow control checks in Data Sent
An endpoint in the Data Sent state MUST NOT need to check flow control limits or send STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames for the stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B9-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0011.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_AllowsRetransmissionWithoutAdditionalFlowControlAfterDataSent
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0011.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsNewBytesWithoutEmittingBlockedFramesAfterDataSent

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0012 Allow ignoring MAX_STREAM_DATA in Data Sent
An endpoint in the Data Sent state MAY safely ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA frames it receives from its peer for that stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B9-P6-S5
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0012.cs::TryApplyMaxStreamDataFrame_PreservesDataSentAfterStalePeerCredit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0013 Enter Data Recvd after all data is acknowledged
Once all stream data has been successfully acknowledged, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Data Recvd state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B10-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0014 Allow application abandonment of stream transmission
From any state that is Ready, Send, or Data Sent, an application MAY signal that it wishes to abandon transmission of stream data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B11-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0015 Send RESET_STREAM when abandoning transmission
In either case, the endpoint MUST send a RESET_STREAM frame, which causes the stream to enter the Reset Sent state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B11-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0016 Permit RESET_STREAM as the first frame
An endpoint MAY send a RESET_STREAM as the first frame that mentions a stream, causing the sending part of that stream to open and then immediately transition to the Reset Sent state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B12-P9-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P1-0017 Enter Reset Recvd after RESET_STREAM is acknowledged
Once a packet containing a RESET_STREAM has been acknowledged, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Reset Recvd state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.1 RFC9000-S3.1-B13-P10-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0001 Do not track unobservable sending-part states
The receiving part of a stream MUST NOT track states on the sending part that cannot be observed, such as the Ready state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0002 Track data delivery to the application
The receiving part of a stream MUST track the delivery of data to the application, including data that cannot be observed by the sender.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B2-P1-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0003 Create peer-initiated receiving parts on first frame
The receiving part of a stream initiated by a peer MUST be created when the first STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frame is received for that stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B5-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0004 Create bidirectional receiving parts on peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
For bidirectional streams initiated by a peer, receipt of a MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frame for the sending part of the stream MUST also create the receiving part.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B5-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0004.cs::TryReceiveStopSendingFrame_CreatesPeerInitiatedReceivingPartOnFirstFrame

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0005 Start receiving parts in Recv
The initial state for the receiving part of a stream MUST be Recv.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B5-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0006 Enter Recv when the peer-side sending part opens
For a bidirectional stream, the receiving part MUST enter the Recv state when the sending part initiated by the endpoint enters the Ready state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0007 Open bidirectional streams on peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
An endpoint MUST open a bidirectional stream when a MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frame is received from the peer for that stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B7-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0007.cs::TryApplyMaxStreamDataFrame_OpensBidirectionalStreamWhenPeerCreditArrives
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0007.cs::TryReceiveStopSendingFrame_OpensBidirectionalStreamWhenPeerStopsReceiving

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0008 Interpret MAX_STREAM_DATA on an unopened stream as peer opening
Receiving a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame for an unopened stream MUST indicate that the remote peer has opened the stream and is providing flow control credit.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B7-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0009 Interpret STOP_SENDING on an unopened stream as a peer desire to stop reception
Receiving a STOP_SENDING frame for an unopened stream MUST indicate that the remote peer no longer wishes to receive data on this stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B7-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0009.cs::TryReceiveStopSendingFrame_OpensUnopenedPeerBidirectionalStreamAndRecordsPeerAbortIntent
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0009.cs::TryReceiveStopSendingFrame_RejectsLocallyInitiatedUnopenedStreams

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0010 Allow reordered peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
Either frame MAY arrive before a STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame if packets are lost or reordered.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B7-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0011 Open lower-numbered peer streams first
Before a stream is created, all streams of the same type with lower-numbered stream IDs MUST be created.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B8-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0011.cs::TryReceiveStopSendingFrame_OpensLowerNumberedPeerStreamsFirst

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0012 Receive STREAM and STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED in Recv
In the Recv state, the endpoint MUST receive STREAM and STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B9-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0013 Buffer received stream data for ordered delivery
Incoming data MUST be buffered for ordered delivery to the application, including any reassembly needed to place it in the correct order.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B9-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0014 Advertise more receive credit as data is consumed
As data is consumed by the application and buffer space becomes available, the endpoint MUST send MAX_STREAM_DATA frames to allow the peer to send more data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B9-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0015 Recognize final size when FIN arrives
When a STREAM frame with a FIN bit is received, the final size of the stream MUST be known.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B10-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0015.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_MarksFinalSizeKnownWhenFinArrives
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0015.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_LeavesFinalSizeUnknownWithoutFin
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0015.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_MarksFinalSizeKnownOnZeroLengthFin

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0016 Enter Size Known after FIN
The receiving part of the stream MUST then enter the Size Known state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B10-P7-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0016.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_EntersSizeKnownWhenFinArrives

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0017 Enter Data Recvd after all data arrives
Once all data for the stream has been received, the receiving part MUST enter the Data Recvd state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B11-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0017.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_EntersDataRecvdAfterAllDataArrives

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0018 Discard further stream frames after all data is received
After all data has been received, any STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames for the stream MAY be discarded.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B11-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0019 Keep Data Recvd until the application receives the data
The Data Recvd state MUST persist until stream data has been delivered to the application.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B12-P9-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0020 Enter Data Read after delivery to the application
Once stream data has been delivered, the stream MUST enter the Data Read state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B12-P9-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0021 Enter Reset Recvd on RESET_STREAM in Recv or Size Known
Receiving a RESET_STREAM frame in the Recv or Size Known state MUST cause the stream to enter the Reset Recvd state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B13-P10-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0022 Allow interruption of delivery and signal RESET_STREAM
An implementation MAY interrupt delivery of stream data, discard any data that was not consumed, and signal the receipt of the RESET_STREAM.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B15-P12-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0023 Remain in Data Recvd when RESET_STREAM is suppressed
If the RESET_STREAM is suppressed, the receiving part of the stream MUST remain in Data Recvd.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B15-P12-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P2-0024 Enter Reset Read after the application receives reset notification
Once the application receives the signal indicating that the stream was reset, the receiving part of the stream MUST transition to the Reset Read state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.2 RFC9000-S3.2-B16-P13-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0001 Forbid terminal-state transmit frames
A sender MUST NOT send STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frames from a terminal state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0002 Forbid post-reset transmission of STREAM frames
A sender MUST NOT send a STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame for a stream in the Reset Sent state or any terminal state after sending a RESET_STREAM frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0003 Allow delayed receipt of state-changing frames
A receiver MAY receive STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frames in any state due to delayed delivery of packets carrying them.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0004 Limit MAX_STREAM_DATA to Recv
The receiver MUST only send MAX_STREAM_DATA frames in the Recv state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0005 Allow STOP_SENDING except after reset reception
A receiver MAY send a STOP_SENDING frame in any state where it has not received a RESET_STREAM frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P3-0006 Allow delayed receipt of receiver-side frames
A sender MAY receive MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frames in any state as a result of delayed delivery of packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.3 RFC9000-S3.3-B5-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P4-0001 Compose bidirectional streams from send and receive parts
A bidirectional stream MUST be composed of sending and receiving parts.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.4 RFC9000-S3.4-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P4-0002 Allow composite stream-state representations
Implementations MAY represent bidirectional stream states as composites of sending and receiving stream states.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.4 RFC9000-S3.4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P4-0003 Require acknowledgment before closed-state transitions in the example mapping
Any mapping to a closed or half-closed state MUST require data to be acknowledged before the transition.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.4 RFC9000-S3.4-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0001 Allow aborting stream reads with an application error code
If an application is no longer interested in the data it is receiving on a stream, it MAY abort reading the stream and specify an application error code.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0002 Signal aborting reads with STOP_SENDING
If the stream is in the Recv or Size Known state, the transport SHOULD signal aborting reading by sending a STOP_SENDING frame to prompt closure of the stream in the opposite direction.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0003 Count data after STOP_SENDING toward flow control
STREAM frames received after sending a STOP_SENDING frame MUST still be counted toward connection and stream flow control even if they are discarded upon receipt.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0004 Treat STOP_SENDING as a request for RESET_STREAM
A STOP_SENDING frame MUST request that the receiving endpoint send a RESET_STREAM frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0005 Send RESET_STREAM after receiving STOP_SENDING in Ready or Send
An endpoint that receives a STOP_SENDING frame MUST send a RESET_STREAM frame if the stream is in the Ready or Send state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0006 Permit deferred RESET_STREAM in Data Sent
If the stream is in the Data Sent state, the endpoint MAY defer sending the RESET_STREAM frame until the packets containing outstanding data are acknowledged or declared lost.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0007 Prefer RESET_STREAM over retransmission after loss
If any outstanding data is declared lost, the endpoint SHOULD send a RESET_STREAM frame instead of retransmitting the data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B5-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0008 Copy STOP_SENDING error codes when sending RESET_STREAM
An endpoint SHOULD copy the error code from the STOP_SENDING frame to the RESET_STREAM frame it sends.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

Notes:
- The source also permits using any application error code.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0009 Allow ignoring later RESET_STREAM error codes
An endpoint that sends a STOP_SENDING frame MAY ignore the error code in any RESET_STREAM frames subsequently received for that stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0010 Prefer STOP_SENDING only for streams that have not been reset
STOP_SENDING SHOULD only be sent for a stream that has not been reset by the peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0011 Repeat STOP_SENDING after loss
An endpoint SHOULD send another STOP_SENDING frame if a packet containing a previous STOP_SENDING is lost.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0012 Allow RESET_STREAM to terminate one direction of a bidirectional stream
An endpoint MAY terminate one direction of a bidirectional stream by sending a RESET_STREAM frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S3P5-0013 Allow STOP_SENDING to encourage prompt opposite-direction termination
An endpoint MAY encourage prompt termination in the opposite direction by sending a STOP_SENDING frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §3.5 RFC9000-S3.5-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-3.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4-0001 Flow-control streams and the whole connection
Streams MUST be flow controlled both individually and across a connection as a whole.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4 RFC9000-S4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4-0002 Limit bytes on streams and across the connection
A QUIC receiver MUST control the maximum amount of data the sender can send on a stream and across all streams at any time.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4 RFC9000-S4-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4-0003 Control how many streams a peer can initiate
A QUIC endpoint MUST control the maximum cumulative number of streams that its peer can initiate.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4 RFC9000-S4-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4-0004 Exclude CRYPTO frames from stream-style flow control
Data sent in CRYPTO frames MUST NOT be flow controlled in the same way as stream data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4 RFC9000-S4-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4-0005 Expose cryptographic buffering limits
QUIC implementations SHOULD provide an interface for the cryptographic protocol implementation to communicate its buffering limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4 RFC9000-S4-B4-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0001 Use limit-based flow control
QUIC MUST employ a limit-based flow control scheme in which a receiver advertises the total bytes it is prepared to receive on a given stream or for the entire connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0001.cs::TryReadStreamData_AdvertisesTotalBytesPreparedToReceive

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0002 Limit per-stream receive buffer use
Stream flow control MUST prevent a single stream from consuming the entire receive buffer for a connection by limiting the amount of data that can be sent on each stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B3-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
