## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0003 Limit total stream-data bytes on the connection
Connection flow control MUST prevent senders from exceeding a receiver's buffer capacity for the connection by limiting the total bytes of stream data sent in STREAM frames on all streams.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B4-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0003.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_LimitsTheTotalStreamDataBytesAcrossTheConnection

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0004 Never send beyond either flow-control limit
Senders MUST NOT send data in excess of either limit.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B5-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0004.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsBytesBeyondConnectionSendLimit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0004.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_RejectsBytesBeyondStreamSendLimit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0005 Set initial flow-control limits in transport parameters
A receiver MUST set initial limits for all streams through transport parameters during the handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0005.cs::TryParseAndFormatTransportParameters_PreservesInitialFlowControlLimits

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0006 Advertise larger flow-control limits with MAX frames
Subsequently, a receiver MUST send MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frames to advertise larger limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B6-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0006.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_AdvertisesIncreasingLimits

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0007 Advertise larger stream limits with MAX_STREAM_DATA
A receiver MAY advertise a larger limit for a stream by sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame with the corresponding stream ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B7-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0011.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_AdvertisesLargerAdvertisedLimits

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0008 Make MAX_STREAM_DATA indicate an absolute stream offset
A MAX_STREAM_DATA frame MUST indicate the maximum absolute byte offset of a stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B7-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0011.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_AdvertisesLargerAdvertisedLimits

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009 Advertise larger connection limits with MAX_DATA
A receiver MAY advertise a larger limit for a connection by sending a MAX_DATA frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B8-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009.cs::TryApplyMaxDataFrame_AdvertisesLargerConnectionLimit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009.cs::TryApplyMaxDataFrame_IgnoresSmallerConnectionLimit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009.cs::TryApplyMaxDataFrame_AcceptsTheMaximumRepresentableConnectionLimit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0010 Track cumulative bytes for connection flow control
A receiver MUST maintain a cumulative sum of bytes received on all streams to check for violations of the advertised connection or stream data limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B8-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0010.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_TracksConnectionReceivedBytesAcrossStreams

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0011 Ignore smaller advertised limits
Once a receiver advertises a limit for the connection or a stream, advertising a smaller limit MUST have no effect rather than be treated as an error.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B9-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0011.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_IgnoresSmallerAdvertisedLimits
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0007.cs::TryApplyMaxStreamDataFrame_AdvertisesLargerLimit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009.cs::TryApplyMaxDataFrame_AdvertisesLargerConnectionLimit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0012 Close on flow-control violations
A receiver MUST close the connection with an error of type FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR if the sender violates the advertised connection or stream data limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B10-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0013 Ignore non-increasing MAX frames
A sender MUST ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frames that do not increase flow control limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B11-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0014 Signal blocked senders
A sender SHOULD send a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame to indicate to the receiver that it has data to write but is blocked by flow control limits.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B12-P9-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0014.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_GeneratesBlockedFramesWhenFlowControlLimitsAreReached

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0015 Periodically signal blocking while flow-control limited
A sender that is flow control limited SHOULD periodically send a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame when it has no ack-eliciting packets in flight.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.1 RFC9000-S4.1-B12-P9-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0001 Allow repeated or early credit advertisements
A receiver MAY send a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame multiple times within a round trip or send it early enough to allow time for loss of the frame and subsequent recovery.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.2 RFC9000-S4.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0001.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_AllowsRepeatedCreditAdvertisements
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0001.cs::TryApplyMaxFrames_IgnoresRepeatedCreditAdvertisementsThatDoNotIncreaseTheLimit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0002 Allow autotuning of flow-control credit
A receiver MAY use an autotuning mechanism to tune the frequency and amount of advertised additional credit based on a round-trip time estimate and the rate at which the receiving application consumes data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.2 RFC9000-S4.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0002.cs::TryReadStreamData_UsesApplicationConsumptionAsAutotuningInput

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0003 Send flow-control frames opportunistically
An endpoint MAY send frames related to flow control only when there are other frames to send, ensuring that flow control does not cause extra packets to be sent.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.2 RFC9000-S4.2-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0003.cs::TryReadStreamData_MakesCreditFramesAvailableForOpportunisticSerialization

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0004 Do not require blocked senders to signal blocking
A blocked sender MUST NOT be required to send STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.2 RFC9000-S4.2-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0004.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_ResumesAfterCreditRestorationWithoutAnyBlockedSignalExchange

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0005 Do not wait for blocked signals before sending credit
A receiver MUST NOT wait for a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame before sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.2 RFC9000-S4.2-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0005.cs::TryReadStreamData_SendsCreditWithoutWaitingForBlockedSignals

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P4-0001 Tear down state and ignore later data after RESET_STREAM
On receipt of a RESET_STREAM frame, an endpoint MUST tear down state for the matching stream and ignore further data arriving on that stream.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.4 RFC9000-S4.4-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P4-0002 Terminate only one direction on RESET_STREAM
RESET_STREAM MUST terminate one direction of a stream abruptly.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.4 RFC9000-S4.4-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P4-0003 Leave the opposite direction unaffected
For a bidirectional stream, RESET_STREAM MUST have no effect on data flow in the opposite direction.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.4 RFC9000-S4.4-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P4-0004 Keep flow-control state for the unterminated direction
Both endpoints MUST maintain flow control state for the stream in the unterminated direction until that direction enters a terminal state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.4 RFC9000-S4.4-B4-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0001 Communicate final size reliably
A sender MUST always communicate the final size of a stream to the receiver reliably, no matter how the stream is terminated.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0001.cs::TryReceiveStreamFrame_CommunicatesFinalSizeWhenTerminatedWithFin
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0001.cs::TryReceiveResetStreamFrame_CommunicatesFinalSizeWhenTerminatedWithReset
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0001.cs::TryReserveSendCapacity_CommunicatesFinalSizeWhenTerminatedWithFin
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0001.cs::TryAbortLocalStreamWrites_CommunicatesFinalSizeWhenTerminatedWithReset

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0002 Use STREAM FIN to carry final size
The final size of a stream MUST be the sum of the Offset and Length fields of a STREAM frame with a FIN flag, including any implicit values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0003 Use RESET_STREAM Final Size to carry final size
The Final Size field of a RESET_STREAM frame MUST carry the final size value.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0003.cs::TryParseResetStreamFrame_ParsesAndFormatsTheFinalSizeField
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0003.cs::TryParseResetStreamFrame_RejectsTruncatedInputs
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0003.cs::TryParseResetStreamFrame_RoundTripsTheMaximumFinalSizeValue

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0004 Account final size at connection level
The receiver MUST use the final size of the stream to account for all bytes sent on the stream in its connection-level flow controller.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0005 Never send data at or beyond final size
An endpoint MUST NOT send data on a stream at or beyond the final size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0006 Make final size immutable once known
Once a final size for a stream is known, it MUST NOT change.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0007 Treat final-size changes as errors
If a RESET_STREAM or STREAM frame is received indicating a change in the final size for the stream, an endpoint SHOULD respond with an error of type FINAL_SIZE_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P5-0008 Treat data beyond final size as an error
A receiver SHOULD treat receipt of data at or beyond the final size as an error of type FINAL_SIZE_ERROR, even after a stream is closed.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.5 RFC9000-S4.5-B6-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0001 Limit the cumulative number of incoming streams
An endpoint MUST limit the cumulative number of incoming streams its peer can open.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0002 Restrict stream IDs to the advertised limit
Only streams with stream IDs less than max_streams * 4 + first_stream_id_of_type MAY be opened.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0003 Set initial stream limits in transport parameters
Initial limits MUST be set in the transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0004 Advertise later stream limits with MAX_STREAMS
Subsequent limits MUST be advertised using MAX_STREAMS frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B2-P1-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0005 Apply separate limits by stream direction
Separate limits MUST apply to unidirectional and bidirectional streams.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B2-P1-S5
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0006 Close on oversized stream-count values in transport parameters
If an oversized max_streams value is received in a transport parameter, the connection MUST be closed immediately with TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0007 Close on oversized stream-count values in frames
If an oversized max_streams value is received in a frame, the connection MUST be closed immediately with FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0008 Do not exceed the peer's stream limit
Endpoints MUST NOT exceed the limit set by their peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0009 Treat over-limit stream IDs as STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR
An endpoint that receives a frame with a stream ID exceeding the limit it has sent MUST treat this as a connection error of type STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0010 Ignore smaller advertised stream limits
Once a receiver advertises a stream limit using the MAX_STREAMS frame, advertising a smaller limit MUST have no effect.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0011 Ignore non-increasing MAX_STREAMS frames
MAX_STREAMS frames that do not increase the stream limit MUST be ignored.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0012 Signal blocked stream openings with STREAMS_BLOCKED
An endpoint that is unable to open a new stream due to the peer's limits SHOULD send a STREAMS_BLOCKED frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0013 Do not wait for STREAMS_BLOCKED before advertising more credit
An endpoint MUST NOT wait to receive STREAMS_BLOCKED before advertising additional credit.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §4.6 RFC9000-S4.6-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-4.6
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0013.cs::TryPeekPeerStreamCapacityRelease_OffersMoreUnidirectionalCreditAfterThePeerStreamCloses
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P6-0013.cs::TryPeekPeerStreamCapacityRelease_ReturnsFalseWhileThePeerStreamIsStillOpen

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0001 Treat a QUIC connection as shared client-server state
A QUIC connection MUST be shared state between a client and a server.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0002 Start every connection with a handshake phase
Each connection MUST start with a handshake phase during which the two endpoints establish a shared secret using the cryptographic handshake protocol and negotiate the application protocol.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0003 Use the handshake to confirm willingness and establish parameters
The handshake MUST confirm that both endpoints are willing to communicate and establish parameters for the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0004 Allow limited connection use during the handshake
An application protocol MAY use the connection during the handshake phase with some limitations.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0005 Allow 0-RTT client data before server response
0-RTT MUST allow application data to be sent by a client before receiving a response from the server.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0006 Do not provide replay protection for 0-RTT
0-RTT MUST NOT provide protection against replay attacks.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B4-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0007 Allow server data before final handshake messages
A server MAY send application data to a client before it receives the final cryptographic handshake messages that allow it to confirm the identity and liveness of the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B4-P3-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5-0008 Use connection IDs to enable migration
Connection IDs MUST allow connections to migrate to a new network path, both as a direct choice of an endpoint and when forced by a change in a middlebox.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5 RFC9000-S5-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0001 Assign each connection a set of CIDs
Each connection MUST possess a set of connection IDs, each of which can identify the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0001.cs::TryParseNewConnectionIdFrame_ExposesMultipleConnectionIdsForTheSameConnection

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0002 Let endpoints choose peer-used connection IDs
Connection IDs MUST be independently selected by endpoints, with each endpoint selecting the connection IDs that its peer uses.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0002.cs::TryParseLongHeader_ExposesTheHandshakeConnectionIds

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0003 Use CIDs to survive address changes
Connection IDs MUST ensure that changes in lower-layer addressing do not cause packets for a QUIC connection to be delivered to the wrong endpoint.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0003.cs::TryParseShortHeader_PreservesTheOpaqueConnectionIdRemainder

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0004 Make CID selection routable and recognizable
Each endpoint MUST select connection IDs using an implementation-specific method that allows packets with that connection ID to be routed back to the endpoint and to be identified by the endpoint upon receipt.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0004.cs::TryFormatVersionNegotiationResponse_EchoesTheClientConnectionIds

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0005 Use multiple CIDs for privacy
Endpoints MUST use multiple connection IDs so that they can send packets that an observer cannot identify as belonging to the same connection without cooperation from an endpoint.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0006 Keep CIDs free of correlating information
Connection IDs MUST NOT contain any information that can be used by an external observer to correlate them with other connection IDs for the same connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0007 Do not issue the same CID twice on a connection
The same connection ID MUST NOT be issued more than once on the same connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0008 Include CID fields in long headers
Packets with long headers MUST include Source Connection ID and Destination Connection ID fields.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicHeaderFuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_LongHeaderParsing_RoundTripsValidInputsAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicHeaderPropertyTests.cs::TryParseLongHeader_RoundTripsHeaderFields
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicLongHeaderPacketTests.cs::TryParseLongHeader_RoundTripsLengthEncodedConnectionIdsAndPayload

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0009 Include only the destination CID in short headers
Packets with short headers MUST include only the Destination Connection ID and omit the explicit length.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0009.cs::TryParseShortHeader_PreservesTrailingBytesWithoutAnExplicitLengthField
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0009.cs::TryParseShortHeader_RejectsLongHeaderPackets
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0009.cs::TryParseShortHeader_AcceptsTheShortestValidShortHeader

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0010 Know the short-header destination CID length
The length of the Destination Connection ID field MUST be known to endpoints.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0011 Allow load balancers to agree on CID handling
Endpoints using a load balancer that routes based on connection ID MAY agree with the load balancer on a fixed length for connection IDs or on an encoding scheme.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0012 Echo client CIDs in Version Negotiation
A Version Negotiation packet MUST echo the connection IDs selected by the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicHeaderFuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_VersionNegotiationParsing_RoundTripsValidInputsAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicHeaderPropertyTests.cs::TryParseVersionNegotiation_RoundTripsSupportedVersions
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicVersionNegotiationPacketTests.cs::TryParseVersionNegotiation_ExposesSupportedVersions

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0013 Permit zero-length CIDs when routing does not need them
A zero-length connection ID MAY be used when a connection ID is not needed to route to the correct endpoint.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0014 Avoid concurrent zero-length-CID connections on the same address tuple
An endpoint MUST NOT use the same IP address and port for multiple concurrent connections with zero-length connection IDs unless it is certain that peer connection migration, NAT rebinding, and client port reuse are not in use.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B9-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0015 Maintain a supply of peer-usable connection IDs
When an endpoint uses a non-zero-length connection ID, it MUST ensure that the peer has a supply of connection IDs from which to choose for packets sent to the endpoint.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1 RFC9000-S5.1-B10-P9-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0015.cs::TryFormatTransportParameters_AdvertisesActiveConnectionIdLimit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0015.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_RejectsActiveConnectionIdLimitBelowTwo
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1-0015.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_AcceptsMinimumActiveConnectionIdLimit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0001 Associate each CID with a sequence number
Each connection ID MUST have an associated sequence number to assist in detecting when NEW_CONNECTION_ID or RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames refer to the same value.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0002 Carry the initial CID in the long-header Source CID field
The initial connection ID issued by an endpoint MUST be sent in the Source Connection ID field of the long packet header during the handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0003 Assign sequence number 0 to the initial CID
The sequence number of the initial connection ID MUST be 0.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0004 Assign sequence number 1 to the preferred-address CID
If the preferred_address transport parameter is sent, the sequence number of the supplied connection ID MUST be 1.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B2-P1-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0005 Use NEW_CONNECTION_ID for additional CIDs
Additional connection IDs MUST be communicated to the peer using NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_FrameCodecPart4_RoundTripsRepresentativeFrameShapesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseNewConnectionIdFrame_AcceptsBoundaryConnectionIdLengths
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseNewConnectionIdFrame_ParsesAndFormatsTheEncodedFields

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0006 Increment CID sequence numbers by one
The sequence number on each newly issued connection ID MUST increase by 1.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0007 Do not sequence the first client-selected CID or Retry-provided CID
The connection ID that a client selects for the first Destination Connection ID field it sends and any connection ID provided by a Retry packet MUST NOT be assigned sequence numbers.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0008 Accept packets carrying an issued CID for the connection lifetime
When an endpoint issues a connection ID, it MUST accept packets that carry this connection ID for the duration of the connection or until its peer invalidates the connection ID via a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0009 Treat active CIDs as valid at any time
Connection IDs that are issued and not retired MUST be considered active and valid for use with the current connection at any time in any packet type.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0010 Try to keep enough unused CIDs available
An endpoint SHOULD ensure that its peer has a sufficient number of available and unused connection IDs.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0011 Advertise active CID limits with transport parameters
Endpoints MUST advertise the number of active connection IDs they are willing to maintain using the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersFuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_TransportParameters_RoundTripsRepresentativeValuesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryFormatTransportParameters_EmitsActiveConnectionIdLimitWhenSendingAsClient
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryFormatTransportParameters_WritesExactTupleSequence
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_RoundTripsKnownFieldsAndPreferredAddress

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0012 Do not exceed the peer's active CID limit
An endpoint MUST NOT provide more connection IDs than the peer's limit.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0013 Allow temporary CID excess only with immediate retirement requirements
An endpoint MAY send connection IDs that temporarily exceed a peer's limit if the NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame also requires the retirement of any excess by including a sufficiently large value in the Retire Prior To field.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B5-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0014 Close on too many active CIDs
After processing a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame and adding and retiring active connection IDs, if the number of active connection IDs exceeds the value advertised in the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter, an endpoint MUST close the connection with an error of type CONNECTION_ID_LIMIT_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0015 Supply a new CID when the peer retires one
An endpoint SHOULD supply a new connection ID when the peer retires a connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0016 May replenish CIDs on previously unused packet receipt
If an endpoint provided fewer connection IDs than the peer's active_connection_id_limit, it MAY supply a new connection ID when it receives a packet with a previously unused connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0017 Allow limiting the total number of issued CIDs
An endpoint MAY limit the total number of connection IDs issued for each connection to avoid the risk of running out of connection IDs.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0018 Allow limiting CID issuance to reduce per-path state
An endpoint MAY also limit the issuance of connection IDs to reduce the amount of per-path state it maintains.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B7-P6-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0019 Keep enough peer-usable CIDs for migration
An endpoint that initiates migration and requires non-zero-length connection IDs SHOULD ensure that the pool of connection IDs available to its peer allows the peer to use a new connection ID on migration.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0020 Forbid new CIDs after selecting zero-length CID in the handshake
An endpoint that selects a zero-length connection ID during the handshake MUST NOT issue a new connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P1-0021 Use zero-length destination CIDs toward such endpoints
A zero-length Destination Connection ID field MUST be used in all packets sent toward such an endpoint over any network path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.1 RFC9000-S5.1.1-B9-P8-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0001 Allow switching to another available CID
An endpoint MAY change the connection ID it uses for a peer to another available one at any time during the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0002 Consume CIDs in response to migration
An endpoint MAY consume connection IDs in response to a migrating peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0003 Maintain a set of peer-issued CIDs
An endpoint MUST maintain a set of connection IDs received from its peer for use when sending packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0004 Retire a CID by sending RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID
When the endpoint wishes to remove a connection ID from use, it MUST send a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame to its peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_FrameCodecPart4_RoundTripsRepresentativeFrameShapesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseRetireConnectionIdFrame_ParsesAndFormatsTheSequenceNumber
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseRetireConnectionIdFrame_RejectsTruncatedInput

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0005 Make RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID a no-reuse request
Sending a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame MUST indicate that the connection ID will not be used again and request that the peer replace it with a new connection ID using a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_FrameCodecPart4_RoundTripsRepresentativeFrameShapesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseRetireConnectionIdFrame_ParsesAndFormatsTheSequenceNumber
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseRetireConnectionIdFrame_RejectsTruncatedInput

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0006 Limit a CID to one local and one destination address
Endpoints MUST limit the use of a connection ID to packets sent from a single local address to a single destination address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0007 Retire CIDs when their address pair is no longer active
Endpoints SHOULD retire connection IDs when they are no longer actively using either the local or destination address for which the connection ID was used.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0008 Prompt peer retirement with an increased Retire Prior To
An endpoint MAY cause its peer to retire connection IDs by sending a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame with an increased Retire Prior To field.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_FrameCodecPart4_RoundTripsRepresentativeFrameShapesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseNewConnectionIdFrame_AcceptsBoundaryConnectionIdLengths
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart4Tests.cs::TryParseNewConnectionIdFrame_ParsesAndFormatsTheEncodedFields

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0009 Continue accepting retired CIDs until the peer retires them
The endpoint SHOULD continue to accept the previously issued connection IDs until they are retired by the peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0010 Allow closing when indicated CIDs can no longer be processed
If the endpoint can no longer process the indicated connection IDs, it MAY close the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B5-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0011 Stop using CIDs after a larger Retire Prior To value
Upon receipt of an increased Retire Prior To field, the peer MUST stop using the corresponding connection IDs and retire them with RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames before adding the newly provided connection ID to the set of active connection IDs.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0012 Limit locally retired but unacknowledged RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames
An endpoint SHOULD limit the number of connection IDs it has retired locally for which RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames have not yet been acknowledged.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0013 Track at least twice the active CID limit
An endpoint SHOULD allow for sending and tracking a number of RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames of at least twice the value of the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0014 Never forget a CID without retiring it
An endpoint MUST NOT forget a connection ID without retiring it.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0015 Allow treating excessive unretired CIDs as an error
An endpoint MAY treat having connection IDs in need of retirement that exceed this limit as a connection error of type CONNECTION_ID_LIMIT_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P1P2-0016 Avoid premature Retire Prior To updates
Endpoints SHOULD NOT issue updates of the Retire Prior To field before receiving RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames that retire all connection IDs indicated by the previous Retire Prior To value.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.1.2 RFC9000-S5.1.2-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.1.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0001 Classify incoming packets on receipt
Incoming packets MUST be classified on receipt.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicHeaderPropertyTests.cs::TryClassifyHeaderForm_UsesTheFirstByteHighBit
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicPacketParserTests.cs::TryClassifyHeaderForm_UsesTheFirstByteHighBit

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0002 Allow existing-connection association or new server connections
Packets MAY either be associated with an existing connection or, for servers, potentially create a new connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0003 Try to associate packets with an existing connection
Endpoints MUST try to associate a packet with an existing connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0004 Process packets that match the existing CID
If the packet has a non-zero-length Destination Connection ID corresponding to an existing connection, QUIC MUST process that packet accordingly.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0005 Process zero-length-CID packets by address match
If the Destination Connection ID is zero length and the addressing information in the packet matches the addressing information the endpoint uses to identify a connection with a zero-length connection ID, QUIC MUST process the packet as part of that connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0006 Allow fragile zero-length-CID matching strategies
An endpoint MAY use just the destination IP and port or both source and destination addresses for identification.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0007 Allow Stateless Reset for unattributed packets
Endpoints MAY send a Stateless Reset for any packets that cannot be attributed to an existing connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0007.cs::CanSendStatelessReset_AllowsShorterResponsesForUnattributedPackets
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0007.cs::CanSendStatelessReset_RejectsResponsesThatAreNotSmallerWithoutLoopPreventionState
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0007.cs::CanSendStatelessReset_AllowsTheMinimumDatagramLengthWhenItIsStillSmallerThanTheTriggeringPacket

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0008 Discard packets inconsistent with connection state
Packets that are matched to an existing connection MUST be discarded if the packets are inconsistent with the state of that connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0009 Discard packets with a different protocol version
Packets MUST be discarded if they indicate a different protocol version than that of the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0010 Discard packets if packet protection cannot be removed
Packets MUST be discarded if removal of packet protection is unsuccessful once the expected keys are available.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0011 Allow discarding weakly protected invalid packets
Invalid packets that lack strong integrity protection, such as Initial, Retry, or Version Negotiation, MAY be discarded.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0012 Generate a connection error on pre-error processing
An endpoint MUST generate a connection error if it processes the contents of these packets before discovering an error.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2-0013 Revert changes made while processing weakly protected invalid packets
An endpoint MUST fully revert any changes made during that processing.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2 RFC9000-S5.2-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P1-0001 Match client packets by client-selected CID
Valid packets sent to clients MUST always include a Destination Connection ID that matches a value the client selects.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.1 RFC9000-S5.2.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P1-0002 Allow zero-length CID matching by local address and port
Clients that choose to receive zero-length connection IDs MAY use the local address and port to identify a connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.1 RFC9000-S5.2.1-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P1-0003 Discard packets that do not match an existing client connection
Packets that do not match an existing connection based on Destination Connection ID or, if that value is zero length, local IP address and port MUST be discarded.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.1 RFC9000-S5.2.1-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P1-0004 Allow clients to drop or buffer not-yet-decryptable packets
A client MAY drop packets encrypted with a key it has not yet computed or buffer them in anticipation of later packets that allow it to compute the key.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.1 RFC9000-S5.2.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P1-0005 Discard packets with a different selected version
If a client receives a packet that uses a different version than it initially selected, it MUST discard that packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.1 RFC9000-S5.2.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0001 Send Version Negotiation for large unsupported packets
If a server receives a packet that indicates an unsupported version and the packet is large enough to initiate a new connection for any supported version, the server SHOULD send a Version Negotiation packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0002 Allow limiting Version Negotiation responses
A server MAY limit the number of packets to which it responds with a Version Negotiation packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0003 Drop smaller unsupported-version packets
Servers MUST drop smaller packets that specify unsupported versions.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0004 Prefer Version Negotiation when possible
Servers SHOULD respond with a Version Negotiation packet, provided that the datagram is sufficiently long.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B3-P2-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicVersionNegotiationTests.cs::ShouldSendVersionNegotiation_RequiresAnUnsupportedClientVersionAndSufficientDatagramSize

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0005 Match supported-version packets by connection ID or address tuple
Packets with a supported version or no Version field MUST be matched to a connection using the connection ID or, for packets with zero-length connection IDs, the local address and port.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0006 Proceed with the handshake on a conforming Initial packet
If the packet is an Initial packet fully conforming with the specification, the server MUST proceed with the handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0007 Refuse new connections with CONNECTION_REFUSED when appropriate
If a server refuses to accept a new connection, it SHOULD send an Initial packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with error code CONNECTION_REFUSED.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0008 Allow limited buffering of 0-RTT packets
If the packet is a 0-RTT packet, the server MAY buffer a limited number of these packets in anticipation of a late-arriving Initial packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0009 Ignore premature Handshake packets from clients
Servers SHOULD ignore any Handshake packets sent before the client receives a server response.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P2-0010 Drop all other incoming packets
Servers MUST drop incoming packets under all other circumstances.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.2 RFC9000-S5.2.2-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0001 Allow out-of-band forwarding for simple load balancers
Servers MAY use an out-of-band mechanism to forward packets to the correct server based on connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B3-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0002 Allow preferred-address migration for dedicated server addresses
If servers can use a dedicated server IP address or port other than the one that the client initially connects to, they MAY use the preferred_address transport parameter to request that clients move connections to that dedicated address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B4-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersFuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_TransportParameters_RoundTripsRepresentativeValuesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_AcceptsPreferredAddressWithZeroedIpv4Family
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_RoundTripsKnownFieldsAndPreferredAddress

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0003 Allow clients to decline preferred addresses
Clients MAY choose not to use the preferred address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B4-P0-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0004 Signal lack of migration support with disable_active_migration
A server in a deployment that does not implement a solution to maintain connection continuity when the client address changes SHOULD indicate that migration is not supported by using the disable_active_migration transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B5-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersFuzzTests.cs::Fuzz_TransportParameters_RoundTripsRepresentativeValuesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryFormatTransportParameters_WritesExactTupleSequence
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicTransportParametersTests.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_RoundTripsKnownFieldsAndPreferredAddress

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0005 Do not let disable_active_migration block preferred-address migration
The disable_active_migration transport parameter MUST NOT prohibit connection migration after a client has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B5-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0005.cs::ServerMigratesToThePreferredAddressAfterValidationEvenWhenDisableActiveMigrationIsSet
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0005.cs::ServerKeepsTheOriginalAddressBeforePreferredAddressValidationCompletesEvenWhenDisableActiveMigrationIsSet
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0005.cs::ServerMigratesToAPortOnlyPreferredAddressEvenWhenDisableActiveMigrationIsSet

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P2P3-0006 Avoid stateless reset oracles in simple load balancing
Server deployments that use this simple form of load balancing MUST avoid the creation of a stateless reset oracle.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §5.2.3 RFC9000-S5.2.3-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.2.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0001 Allow clients to open connections
When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY open a connection, which begins the exchange described in Section 7.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B4-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0001.cs::ConnectAsync_OpensARealLoopbackConnectionFromTheClientRole

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0002 Allow clients to enable Early Data
When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY enable Early Data when available.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B5-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0003 Allow clients to learn Early Data acceptance
When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY be informed when Early Data has been accepted or rejected by a server.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B6-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0004 Allow servers to listen for connections
When implementing the server role, an application protocol MAY listen for incoming connections, which prepares for the exchange described in Section 7.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B8-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0004.cs::ListenAsync_OpensARealLoopbackListenerForTheServerRole

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0005 Allow servers to embed application data in resumption tickets
If Early Data is supported, an application protocol MAY embed application-controlled data in the TLS resumption ticket sent to the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B9-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0006 Allow servers to use resumption-ticket data for Early Data decisions
If Early Data is supported, an application protocol MAY retrieve application-controlled data from the client's resumption ticket and accept or reject Early Data based on that information.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §5.3 RFC9000-S5.3-B10-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-5.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0007 Allow configuring initial stream counts
In either role, an application protocol MAY configure minimum values for the initial number of permitted streams of each type as communicated in the transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0008 Allow receive-buffer control via flow control limits
In either role, an application protocol MAY control resource allocation for receive buffers by setting flow control limits both for streams and for the connection.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0009 Allow handshake status queries
In either role, an application protocol MAY identify whether the handshake has completed successfully or is still ongoing.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S5P3-0010 Allow connection liveness maintenance
In either role, an application protocol MAY keep a connection from silently closing by either generating PING frames or requesting that the transport send additional frames before the idle timeout expires.

In either role, an application protocol MAY immediately close the connection.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S6-0001 Size the first datagram for multi-version support
Clients that support multiple QUIC versions SHOULD ensure that the first UDP datagram they send is sized to the largest of the minimum datagram sizes from all versions they support, using PADDING frames as necessary.

Clients that support multiple QUIC versions SHOULD ensure that the first UDP datagram they send is sized to the largest of the minimum datagram sizes from all versions they support, using PADDING frames (Section 19.1) as necessary.

An endpoint MUST NOT send a Version Negotiation packet in response to receiving a Version Negotiation packet.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S6P2-0001 Reject unsupported Version Negotiation attempts
A client that supports only this version of QUIC MUST abandon the current connection attempt if it receives a Version Negotiation packet unless it has received and successfully processed any other packet or the Version Negotiation packet lists the QUIC version selected by the client.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicVersionNegotiationTests.cs::ShouldDiscardVersionNegotiation_RespectsPreviouslyProcessedPacketsAndSelectedVersions

A client that supports only this version of QUIC MUST abandon the current connection attempt if it receives a Version Negotiation packet, with the following two exceptions.

A client MUST discard a Version Negotiation packet that lists the QUIC version selected by the client.
