Every connection MUST produce distinct and unrelated keys.

Keying material from the cryptographic handshake MUST be usable for packet protection for both 0-RTT and 1-RTT packets.

The cryptographic handshake MUST provide authenticated exchange of transport parameter values for both endpoints.

The cryptographic handshake MUST provide confidentiality protection for server transport parameters.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

The cryptographic handshake MUST provide authenticated negotiation of an application protocol.

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0007.cs::TryParseLongHeader_AllowsAZeroLengthHandshakeSourceConnectionId

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0008 Keep 0-RTT connection IDs aligned with the first Initial packet
0-RTT packets in the first flight MUST use the same Destination Connection ID and Source Connection ID values as the client's first Initial packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0009 Adopt the server Source Connection ID on first receipt
Upon first receiving an Initial or Retry packet from the server, the client MUST use the Source Connection ID supplied by the server as the Destination Connection ID for subsequent packets, including any 0-RTT packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0010 Once a client has received a valid Initial packet from the server, it MUST discard any subsequent packet it receives on that connection with a different Source Connection ID
Once a client has received a valid Initial packet from the server, it MUST discard any subsequent packet it receives on that connection with a different Source Connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B8-P7-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0011 Change the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets in response to only the first received Initial or Retry packet
A client MUST change the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets in response to only the first received Initial or Retry packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0012 Set the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets based on the first received Initial packet
A server MUST set the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets based on the first received Initial packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B9-P8-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0013 Allow further Destination Connection ID changes only from NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames
An endpoint MAY make further changes to the Destination Connection ID only if the values are taken from NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B9-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P2-0014 Discard subsequent Initial packets with a different Source Connection ID
Subsequent Initial packets that include a different Source Connection ID MUST be discarded.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.2 RFC9000-S7.2-B9-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0001 Authenticate connection ID choices through transport parameters
The choice each endpoint makes about connection IDs during the handshake MUST be authenticated by including all values in transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0002 Echo the client Source Connection ID in initial_source_connection_id
Each endpoint MUST include the value of the Source Connection ID field from the first Initial packet it sent in the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0003 Echo the client Initial Destination Connection ID in original_destination_connection_id
A server MUST include the Destination Connection ID field from the first Initial packet it received from the client in the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0004 Echo the Retry Source Connection ID when Retry is used
If it sends a Retry packet, a server MUST also include the Source Connection ID field from the Retry packet in the retry_source_connection_id transport parameter.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0005 The values provided by a peer for these transport parameters MUST match the values that an endpoint used in the Destination and Source Connection ID fields of Initial packets that it sent (and received, for servers)
The values provided by a peer for these transport parameters MUST match the values that an endpoint used in the Destination and Source Connection ID fields of Initial packets that it sent (and received, for servers).

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0006 Validate that received transport parameters match received connection ID values
Endpoints MUST validate that received transport parameters match received connection ID values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0007 Treat the absence of the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter from either endpoint or the absence of the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter from the server as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
An endpoint MUST treat the absence of the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter from either endpoint or the absence of the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter from the server as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0008 Treat the following as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR or PROTOCOL_VIOLATION:
An endpoint MUST treat the following as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR or PROTOCOL_VIOLATION:

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P3-0009 Include zero-length connection IDs as zero-length transport parameters
If a zero-length connection ID is selected, the corresponding transport parameter MUST be included with a zero-length value.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.3 RFC9000-S7.3-B10-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4-0001 Treat receipt of a transport parameter with an invalid value as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a transport parameter with an invalid value as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4 RFC9000-S7.4-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4-0002 Send a parameter more than once in a given transport parameters extension
An endpoint MUST NOT send a parameter more than once in a given transport parameters extension.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4 RFC9000-S7.4-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4-0003 Treat receipt of duplicate transport parameters as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
An endpoint SHOULD treat receipt of duplicate transport parameters as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.4 RFC9000-S7.4-B8-P7-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0001 The definition of a new transport parameter (Section 7
The definition of a new transport parameter (Section 7.4.2) MUST specify whether storing the transport parameter for 0-RTT is mandatory, optional, or prohibited.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0002 Use remembered values for the following parameters: ack_delay_exponent, max_ack_delay, initial_source_connection_id, original_destination_connection_id, preferred_address, retry_source_connection_id, and stateless_reset_token
A client MUST NOT use remembered values for the following parameters: ack_delay_exponent, max_ack_delay, initial_source_connection_id, original_destination_connection_id, preferred_address, retry_source_connection_id, and stateless_reset_token.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0003 Use the server's new values in the handshake instead
The client MUST use the server's new values in the handshake instead; if the server does not provide new values, the default values are used.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0004 That attempts to send 0-RTT data MUST remember all other transport parameters used by the server that it is able to process
A client that attempts to send 0-RTT data MUST remember all other transport parameters used by the server that it is able to process.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0005 If 0-RTT data is accepted by the server, the server MUST NOT reduce any limits or alter any values that might be violated by the client with its 0-RTT data
If 0-RTT data is accepted by the server, the server MUST NOT reduce any limits or alter any values that might be violated by the client with its 0-RTT data.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0006 In particular, a server that accepts 0-RTT data MUST NOT set values for the following parameters (Section 18
In particular, a server that accepts 0-RTT data MUST NOT set values for the following parameters (Section 18.2) that are smaller than the remembered values of the parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0007 The applicable subset of transport parameters that permit the sending of application data SHOULD be set to non-zero values for 0-RTT
The applicable subset of transport parameters that permit the sending of application data SHOULD be set to non-zero values for 0-RTT.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B15-P7-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0008 Store and recover the previously sent values of the max_idle_timeout, max_udp_payload_size, and disable_active_migration parameters and reject 0-RTT if it selects smaller values
A server MAY store and recover the previously sent values of the max_idle_timeout, max_udp_payload_size, and disable_active_migration parameters and reject 0-RTT if it selects smaller values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B17-P9-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0009 Reject 0-RTT data if the restored values for transport parameters cannot be supported
A server MUST reject 0-RTT data if the restored values for transport parameters cannot be supported.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B18-P10-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0010 Do not use updated transport-parameter values in 0-RTT
When sending frames in 0-RTT packets, a client MUST NOT use updated values it learns from the server's updated transport parameters or from frames received in 1-RTT packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B19-P11-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0011 Use remembered 0-RTT values only until the handshake completes
When sending frames in 0-RTT packets, a client MUST only use remembered transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B19-P11-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0012 Allow 0-RTT only with remembered transport parameters
Updated values of transport parameters from the handshake MUST apply only to 1-RTT packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B19-P11-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P1-0013 Let updated transport-parameter values in 0-RTT signal a protocol violation
A server MAY treat the use of updated transport parameters in 0-RTT as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.4.1 RFC9000-S7.4.1-B19-P11-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P4P2-0001 Ignore transport parameters that it does not support
An endpoint MUST ignore transport parameters that it does not support.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §7.4.2 RFC9000-S7.4.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P5-0001 Support buffering at least 4096 bytes of data received in out-of-order CRYPTO frames
Implementations MUST support buffering at least 4096 bytes of data received in out-of-order CRYPTO frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.5 RFC9000-S7.5-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P5-0002 Choose to allow more data to be buffered during the handshake
Endpoints MAY choose to allow more data to be buffered during the handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.5 RFC9000-S7.5-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.5
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicCryptoBufferTests.cs::TryAddFrame_AllowsConfiguredCapacityDuringHandshake

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P5-0003 If an endpoint does not expand its buffer, it MUST close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code
If an endpoint does not expand its buffer, it MUST close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.5 RFC9000-S7.5-B4-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P5-0004 Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame, it MAY discard that CRYPTO frame and all CRYPTO frames received in the future, or it MAY close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code
Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame, it MAY discard that CRYPTO frame and all future CRYPTO frames or close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §7.5 RFC9000-S7.5-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-7.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S7P5-0005 Containing discarded CRYPTO frames MUST be acknowledged because the packet has been received and processed by the transport even though the CRYPTO frame was discarded
Packets containing discarded CRYPTO frames MUST be acknowledged because the packet has been received and processed by the transport even though the CRYPTO frame was discarded.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8-0001 Limit sending to unvalidated addresses
After receiving packets from an address that is not yet validated, an endpoint MUST limit the amount of data it sends to the unvalidated address to three times the amount of data received from that address.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0005 If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit
If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit; see Section 8.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0006 To protect against such attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited
To protect against such attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0007 Servers SHOULD ensure that tokens sent in Retry packets are only accepted for a short time, as they are returned immediately by clients
Servers SHOULD ensure that tokens sent in Retry packets are only accepted for a short time, as they are returned immediately by clients.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0008 Tokens that are provided in NEW_TOKEN frames (Section 19
Tokens that are provided in NEW_TOKEN frames (Section 19.7) need to be valid for longer but SHOULD NOT be accepted multiple times.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0009 Servers are encouraged to allow tokens to be used only once, if possible
Servers are encouraged to allow tokens to be used only once, if possible; tokens MAY include additional information about clients to further narrow applicability or reuse.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0010 Make address-validation tokens hard to guess and integrity protected
An address validation token MUST be difficult to guess and, for token-based schemes, covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1P4-0011 Preserve anti-amplification if the client address changes
If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit.

To protect against replay attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited.

An endpoint MAY send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames to guard against packet loss.

However, an endpoint SHOULD NOT send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames in a single packet.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P1-0003 Probe a new path with packets containing a PATH_CHALLENGE frame more frequently than it would send an Initial packet
An endpoint SHOULD NOT probe a new path with packets containing a PATH_CHALLENGE frame more frequently than it would send an Initial packet.

The endpoint MUST use unpredictable data in every PATH_CHALLENGE frame so that it can associate the peer's response with the corresponding PATH_CHALLENGE.

An endpoint MUST expand datagrams that contain a PATH_CHALLENGE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes, unless the anti-amplification limit for the path does not permit sending a datagram of this size.

To ensure that the path MTU is large enough, the endpoint MUST perform a second path validation by sending a PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a datagram of at least 1200 bytes.

To initiate path validation, an endpoint MUST send a PATH_CHALLENGE frame containing an unpredictable payload on the path to be validated.

On receiving a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, an endpoint MUST respond by echoing the data contained in the PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a PATH_RESPONSE frame.

An endpoint MUST NOT delay transmission of a packet containing a PATH_RESPONSE frame unless constrained by congestion control.

A PATH_RESPONSE frame MUST be sent on the network path where the PATH_CHALLENGE frame was received.

An endpoint MUST expand datagrams that contain a PATH_RESPONSE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P2-0007 Send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame
An endpoint MUST NOT send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame; see Section 13.3.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P2-0008 Limit PATH_RESPONSE fan-out
An endpoint MUST NOT send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P4-0001 Abandon path validation based on a timer
Endpoints SHOULD abandon path validation based on a timer.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P4-0002 That has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error, noting that this is only possible if the network path exists but does not support the required MTU (Section 14)
An endpoint that has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error, noting that this is only possible if the network path exists but does not support the required MTU (Section 14).

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-8.2.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P2P4-0003 Signal no viable path when appropriate
An endpoint that has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0001 Initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed, as defined in Section 4
An endpoint MUST NOT initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed, as defined in Section 4.1.2 of [QUIC-TLS].

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0002 If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint also MUST NOT send packets (including probing packets
If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint also MUST NOT send packets (including probing packets; see Section 9.1) from a different local address to the address the peer used during the handshake, unless the endpoint has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter from the peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0003 If the peer violates this requirement, the endpoint MUST either drop the incoming packets on that path without generating a Stateless Reset or proceed with path validation and allow the peer to migrate
If the peer violates this requirement, the endpoint MUST either drop the incoming packets on that path without generating a Stateless Reset or proceed with path validation and allow the peer to migrate.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0004 Perform path validation (Section 8
An endpoint MUST perform path validation (Section 8.2) if it detects any change to a peer's address, unless it has previously validated that address.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0005 When an endpoint has no validated path on which to send packets, it MAY discard connection state
When an endpoint has no validated path on which to send packets, it MAY discard connection state.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0006 Capable of connection migration MAY wait for a new path to become available before discarding connection state
An endpoint capable of connection migration MAY wait for a new path to become available before discarding connection state.

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0007 If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard these packets
If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard these packets.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0008 Forbid migration before handshake confirmation
An endpoint MUST NOT initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0009 Respect Disable Active Migration
If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint MUST NOT send packets from a different local address to the address the peer used during the handshake unless the endpoint has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter from the peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0010 Validate Peer Address Changes
An endpoint MUST perform path validation if it detects any change to a peer's address unless it has previously validated that address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0011 Restrict Non-Probing Packets Until Peer Reachability Is Established
Servers MUST NOT send non-probing packets toward a client address until they see a non-probing packet from that address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9-0012 Discard Packets from Unknown Server Addresses
If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard those packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9 RFC9000-S9-B7-P6-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P1-0001 Probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation (Section 8
An endpoint MAY probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation (Section 8.2) prior to migrating the connection to the new local address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.1 RFC9000-S9.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P1-0002 Probe New Local Addresses Before Migration
An endpoint MAY probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation before migrating the connection to the new local address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.1 RFC9000-S9.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0001 Defer path validation until after a peer sends the next non-probing frame to its new address
An endpoint MAY defer path validation until after a peer sends the next non-probing frame to its new address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.2 RFC9000-S9.2-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0002 Initiate Path Validation on the New Path
To establish reachability on the new path, an endpoint MUST initiate path validation on the new path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.2 RFC9000-S9.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0003 Reset Congestion State on Migration
When migrating, an endpoint MUST immediately reset its congestion controller and round-trip time estimator for the new path to initial values unless the only change in the peer's address is its port number.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.2 RFC9000-S9.2-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0004 Retain State for Port-Only Changes
When the only change in the peer's address is its port number, an endpoint MAY retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate instead of reverting to initial values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.2 RFC9000-S9.2-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0005 Validate ECN Capability After Migration
An endpoint MUST validate ECN capability as described in Section 13.4 when it migrates to a new path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.2 RFC9000-S9.2-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0001 If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address and MUST initiate path validation (Section 8
If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address and initiate path validation (Section 8.2) to verify the peer's ownership of the address if validation is not already underway.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3 RFC9000-S9.3-B3-P2-S1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0002 Allow data on an unvalidated peer address
An endpoint MAY send data to an unvalidated peer address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0003 Protect unvalidated peer-address traffic
An endpoint MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2 when it sends data to an unvalidated peer address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0004 Skip validation of a peer address if that address has been seen recently
An endpoint MAY skip validation of a peer address if that address has been seen recently.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0005 After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens (Section 8) to the client
After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens (Section 8) to the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3 RFC9000-S9.3-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0006 Send Subsequent Packets to the Migrated Address
If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0007 Initiate Path Validation After Permitting Migration
If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST initiate path validation to verify the peer's ownership of the address if validation is not already underway.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0008 Change Sending Address Only on the Highest-Numbered Non-Probing Packet
An endpoint MUST only change the address to which it sends packets in response to the highest-numbered non-probing packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

An endpoint MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0010 Allow Abandoning Other Path Validation
An endpoint MAY abandon any path validation for other addresses after changing the address to which it sends non-probing packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0011 Send New Address Validation Tokens
After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens to the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P1-0001 Limit Sending Until Address Validation
Until a peer's address is deemed valid, an endpoint MUST limit the amount of data it sends to that address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P2-0001 To protect the connection from failing due to such a spurious migration, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address when validation of a new peer address fails
To protect the connection from failing due to such a spurious migration, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address when validation of a new peer address fails.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.2 RFC9000-S9.3.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P2-0002 If an endpoint has no state about the last validated peer address, it MUST close the connection silently by discarding all connection state
If an endpoint has no state about the last validated peer address, it MUST close the connection silently by discarding all connection state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.2 RFC9000-S9.3.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P2-0003 For instance, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets
For instance, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.2 RFC9000-S9.3.2-B4-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P2-0004 Revert to the Last Validated Peer Address
When validation of a new peer address fails, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.2 RFC9000-S9.3.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P2-0005 Allow Stateless Reset After Silent Close
After discarding all connection state, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.2 RFC9000-S9.3.2-B4-P3-S3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P3-0001 In response to an apparent migration, endpoints MUST validate the previously active path using a PATH_CHALLENGE frame
In response to an apparent migration, endpoints MUST validate the previously active path using a PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.3 RFC9000-S9.3.3-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P3-0002 That receives a PATH_CHALLENGE on an active path SHOULD send a non-probing packet in response
An endpoint that receives a PATH_CHALLENGE on an active path SHOULD send a non-probing packet in response.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.3 RFC9000-S9.3.3-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P3-0003 Return to the Original Path After a New Highest Packet Number
A non-probing packet received on the original path that increases the maximum received packet number MUST cause the endpoint to move back to that path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.3 RFC9000-S9.3.3-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3P3-0004 Use Heuristics for Attack Detection
An endpoint MAY use heuristics to improve detection of this style of attack.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.3.3 RFC9000-S9.3.3-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.3.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0001 Sent on the old path MUST NOT contribute to congestion control or RTT estimation for the new path
Packets sent on the old path MUST NOT contribute to congestion control or RTT estimation for the new path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0002 Use updated congestion state only after confirming the peer address
On confirming a peer's ownership of its new address, an endpoint MUST immediately reset the congestion controller and round-trip time estimator for the new path to initial values unless the only change in the peer's address is its port number.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0003 Retain congestion state for port-only changes
Because port-only changes are commonly the result of NAT rebinding or other middlebox activity, the endpoint MAY instead retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate in those cases instead of reverting to initial values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0004 Keep ACK coverage across multiple paths
A receiver of packets on multiple paths MUST still send ACK frames covering all received packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0005 Retain State for Port-Only Peer Changes
When the only change in the peer's address is its port number, an endpoint MAY retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate instead of reverting to initial values.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0006 ACK All Received Packets Across Paths
A receiver of packets on multiple paths MUST send ACK frames covering all received packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0007 Allow Probe-Packet Loss Exceptions
A sender MAY make exceptions for probe packets so their loss detection is independent and does not unduly cause the congestion controller to reduce its sending rate.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0008 Allow a Separate PATH_CHALLENGE Timer
An endpoint MAY set a separate timer when a PATH_CHALLENGE is sent, and the timer is canceled if the corresponding PATH_RESPONSE is received.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0009 Allow Retrying PATH_CHALLENGE on Timeout
If the timer fires before the PATH_RESPONSE is received, an endpoint MAY send a new PATH_CHALLENGE and restart the timer for a longer period of time.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B6-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0010 Set the PATH_CHALLENGE Timer Conservatively
This timer SHOULD be set as described in Section 6.2.1 of [QUIC-RECOVERY].

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.4 RFC9000-S9.4-B6-P5-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P4-0011 Do Not Make the PATH_CHALLENGE Timer More Aggressive
This timer MUST NOT be more aggressive.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0001 At any time, endpoints MAY change the Destination Connection ID they transmit with to a value that has not been used on another path
At any time, endpoints MAY change the Destination Connection ID they transmit with to a value that has not been used on another path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0002 Prevent connection-id reuse across local addresses
An endpoint MUST NOT reuse a connection ID when sending from more than one local address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0003 Prevent connection-id reuse across destination addresses
Similarly, an endpoint MUST NOT reuse a connection ID when sending to more than one destination address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0004 Due to network changes outside the control of its peer, an endpoint might receive packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, in which case it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address
Due to network changes outside the control of its peer, an endpoint might receive packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, in which case it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0005 Limit use of the zero-length connection ID for migration
An endpoint SHOULD NOT initiate migration with a peer that has requested a zero-length connection ID, because traffic over the new path might be trivially linkable to traffic over the old one.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0006 Keep address changes infrequent
Changing address can cause a peer to reset its congestion control state, so addresses SHOULD only be changed infrequently.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B9-P8-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0007 Provide new connection IDs before peers migrate
To ensure that migration is possible and packets sent on different paths cannot be correlated, endpoints SHOULD provide new connection IDs before peers migrate.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B10-P9-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0008 Keep Provided Connection IDs Unlinkable
Endpoints MUST ensure that connection IDs they provide cannot be linked by any other entity.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0009 Allow Reuse with the Same Local Address
If an endpoint receives packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0010 Avoid Migration with Zero-Length Connection IDs
An endpoint SHOULD NOT initiate migration with a peer that has requested a zero-length connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0011 Change Addresses Infrequently
Addresses SHOULD only be changed infrequently.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B9-P8-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P5-0012 Provide New Connection IDs Before Migration
Endpoints SHOULD provide new connection IDs before peers migrate.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.5 RFC9000-S9.5-B10-P9-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6-0001 If a client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard these packets
If a client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard these packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6 RFC9000-S9.6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6-0002 Discard Unexpected Server-Address Packets
If a client receives packets from a new server address when it has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard those packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6 RFC9000-S9.6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0001 Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family (IPv4 and IPv6) to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment
Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family (IPv4 and IPv6) to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0002 Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation (see Section 8
Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation (see Section 8.2).

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0003 Begin sending packets to the new server address after successful validation
As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD begin sending all future packets to the new server address using the new connection ID.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0004 Discontinue use of the old server address after successful validation
As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD discontinue use of the old server address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0005 Keep using the original server address after failed validation
If path validation fails, the client MUST continue sending all future packets to the server's original IP address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B5-P4-S2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0006 Convey a Preferred Address in the TLS Handshake
A server MUST convey a preferred address by including the preferred_address transport parameter in the TLS handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B2-P1-S1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0007 Allow Per-Family Preferred Addresses
Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0008 Choose a Preferred Address After Handshake Confirmation
Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0009 Use an Unused Active Connection ID for Preferred-Address Migration
A client MUST construct packets using any previously unused active connection ID, taken from either the preferred_address transport parameter or a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P1-0010 Move Future Packets to the New Server Address After Validation
As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD begin sending all future packets to the new server address using the new connection ID and discontinue use of the old server address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.1 RFC9000-S9.6.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0001 That migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating
A client that migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating; see Section 21.5.3.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.2 RFC9000-S9.6.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0002 Send non-probing packets from its original address until it receives a non-probing packet from the client at its preferred address and until the server has validated the new path
The server MUST send non-probing packets from its original address until it receives a non-probing packet from the client at its preferred address and until the server has validated the new path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.2 RFC9000-S9.6.2-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0003 Probe on the path toward the client from its preferred address
The server MUST probe on the path toward the client from its preferred address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.2 RFC9000-S9.6.2-B4-P3-S1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0004 Drop newer packets for this connection that are received on the old IP address
The server SHOULD drop newer packets for this connection that are received on the old IP address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0005 Continue to process delayed packets that are received on the old IP address
The server MAY continue to process delayed packets that are received on the old IP address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0006 Do not use preferred-address values for other connections
The addresses that a server provides in the preferred_address transport parameter MUST be treated as valid only for the connection in which they are provided.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0007 Use these for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection
A client MUST NOT use these for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0008 Validate the Chosen Preferred Address Before Migrating
A client that migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.2 RFC9000-S9.6.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0009 Respond to PATH_CHALLENGE with PATH_RESPONSE
If the received packet contains a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, the server MUST send a packet containing a PATH_RESPONSE frame as described in Section 8.2.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0010 Move Exclusively to the Preferred Address After Validation
Once the server has completed path validation and has received a non-probing packet with a new largest packet number on its preferred address, the server MUST send non-probing packets to the client exclusively from its preferred IP address.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §9.6.2 RFC9000-S9.6.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-9.6.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P2-0011 Restrict Preferred-Address Values to the Current Connection
A client MUST NOT use the addresses provided in the preferred_address transport parameter for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0001 In this case, the client SHOULD perform path validation to both the original and preferred server address from the client's new address concurrently
In this case, the client SHOULD perform path validation to both the original and preferred server address from the client's new address concurrently.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0002 If path validation of the server's preferred address succeeds, the client MUST abandon validation of the original address and migrate to using the server's preferred address
If path validation of the server's preferred address succeeds, the client MUST abandon validation of the original address and migrate to using the server's preferred address.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0003 If path validation of the server's preferred address fails but validation of the server's original address succeeds, the client MAY migrate to its new address and continue sending to the server's original address
If path validation of the server's preferred address fails but validation of the server's original address succeeds, the client MAY migrate to its new address and continue sending to the server's original address.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0004 If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9
If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0005 Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address
Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address; see Section 8.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0006 That migrates to a new address SHOULD use a preferred address from the same address family for the server
A client that migrates to a new address SHOULD use a preferred address from the same address family for the server.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0007 Keep preferred-address connection IDs path-agnostic
The connection ID provided in the `preferred_address` transport parameter MAY be used independently of the addresses that are provided.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0008 Use the preferred-address connection ID on any path
The connection ID is provided to ensure that the client has a connection ID available for migration, but the client MAY use this connection ID on any path.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P6P3-0009 Protect Preferred-Address Traffic from Attacks
If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than was observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address.

The client MAY use the connection ID provided in the preferred_address transport parameter on any path.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P7-0001 That send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437], unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels
Endpoints that send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437], unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P7-0002 The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label, as a stable flow label would enable correlating activity on multiple paths
The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label, as a stable flow label would enable correlating activity on multiple paths; see Section 9.5.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P7-0003 Apply IPv6 Flow Labels
Endpoints that send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437] unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P7-0004 Minimize Flow-Label Linkability
The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10-0001 Discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets
An endpoint MAY discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets; see Section 8.2.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10-0002 Permit discarding unvalidated state
An endpoint MAY discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0001 To avoid excessively small idle timeout periods, endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO)
To avoid excessively small idle timeout periods, endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO).

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0002 Silently close on idle timeout
If a max_idle_timeout is specified by either endpoint in its transport parameters, the connection MUST be silently closed and its state discarded when it remains idle for longer than the minimum of the max_idle_timeout values advertised by both endpoints.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0003 Advertise and compute idle timeout
Each endpoint MUST advertise a `max_idle_timeout`, and the effective value at an endpoint is the minimum of the two advertised values, or the sole advertised value if only one endpoint advertises a non-zero value.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0004 Commit to immediate close before timeout
An endpoint that announces a max_idle_timeout MUST initiate an immediate close if it abandons the connection before the effective timeout expires.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0005 Restart idle timer on received traffic
An endpoint MUST restart its idle timer when a packet from its peer is received and processed successfully.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0006 Restart idle timer on fresh ack-eliciting send
An endpoint MUST also restart its idle timer when it sends an ack-eliciting packet if no other ack-eliciting packets have been sent since it last received and processed a packet.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1-0007 Bound idle timeout by PTO
Endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO).

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1P1-0001 Allow liveness probes
An endpoint MAY send a PING or another ack-eliciting frame to test the connection for liveness if the peer could time out soon.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1P2-0001 Application protocols that use QUIC SHOULD provide guidance on when deferring an idle timeout is appropriate
Application protocols that use QUIC SHOULD provide guidance on when deferring an idle timeout is appropriate.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P1P2-0002 Allow deferring idle timeout
An implementation of QUIC MAY provide applications with an option to defer an idle timeout.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0001 These states SHOULD persist for at least three times the current PTO interval as defined in [QUIC-RECOVERY]
These states SHOULD persist for at least three times the current PTO interval as defined in [QUIC-RECOVERY].

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0002 That have some alternative means to ensure that late-arriving packets do not induce a response, such as those that are able to close the UDP socket, MAY end these states earlier to allow for faster resource recovery
Endpoints that have some alternative means to ensure that late-arriving packets do not induce a response, such as those that are able to close the UDP socket, MAY end these states earlier to allow for faster resource recovery.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0003 Servers that retain an open socket for accepting new connections SHOULD NOT end the closing or draining state early
Servers that retain an open socket for accepting new connections SHOULD NOT end the closing or draining state early.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B7-P6-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0004 Once its closing or draining state ends, an endpoint SHOULD discard all connection state
Once its closing or draining state ends, an endpoint SHOULD discard all connection state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B8-P7-S1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0005 Send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets belonging to this connection
The endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets belonging to this connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B8-P7-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0006 Send CONNECTION_CLOSE to terminate immediately
An endpoint MUST send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame to terminate the connection immediately.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0006.cs::LocalCloseRequested_TransitionsTheRuntimeToClosingAndEmitsConnectionClose

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0007 Close all streams on CONNECTION_CLOSE
A CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MUST cause all streams to immediately become closed.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0008 Reset open streams implicitly
Open streams MUST be implicitly reset when a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame is sent.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

Notes:
- Split from one sentence that also states that all streams become closed.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0009 Enter closing state after sending close
After sending a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, an endpoint MUST immediately enter the closing state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0010 Enter draining state after receiving close
After receiving a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, endpoints MUST enter the draining state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0011 Close immediately on protocol violation
Violations of the protocol MUST lead to an immediate close.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2-0012 Permit immediate close after application shutdown
An immediate close MAY be used after an application protocol has arranged to close a connection.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2 RFC9000-S10.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0001 Limit the rate at which it generates packets in the closing state
An endpoint SHOULD limit the rate at which it generates packets in the closing state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0002 An endpoint's selected connection ID and the QUIC version are sufficient information to identify packets for a closing connection
An endpoint's selected connection ID and the QUIC version are sufficient information to identify packets for a closing connection; the endpoint MAY discard all other connection state.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2.1 RFC9000-S10.2.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0003 Retain packet protection keys for incoming packets to allow it to read and process a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame
An endpoint MAY retain packet protection keys for incoming packets to allow it to read and process a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2.1 RFC9000-S10.2.1-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0004 Drop packet protection keys when entering the closing state and send a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in response to any UDP datagram that is received
An endpoint MAY drop packet protection keys when entering the closing state and send a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in response to any UDP datagram that is received.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2.1 RFC9000-S10.2.1-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0005 To avoid being used for an amplification attack, such endpoints MUST limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to three times the cumulative size of the packets that are received and attributed to the connection
To avoid being used for an amplification attack, such endpoints MUST limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to three times the cumulative size of the packets that are received and attributed to the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §10.2.1 RFC9000-S10.2.1-B6-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S10P2P1-0006 To minimize the state that an endpoint maintains for a closing connection, endpoints MAY send the exact same packet in response to any received packet
To minimize the state that an endpoint maintains for a closing connection, endpoints MAY send the exact same packet in response to any received packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
