  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0039.cs::TryRegisterLoss_DoesNotChangeTheCongestionWindowForIneligibleLossSignals

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4-0001 QUIC endpoints MAY use ECN to detect and respond to network congestion
QUIC endpoints MAY use ECN [RFC3168] to detect and respond to network congestion.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4 RFC9000-S13.4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0001.cs::SenderFlowController_ProcessesEcnInAckFrames

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4-0002 Check whether the path and peer support ECN before enabling it
To enable ECN, a sending QUIC endpoint MUST first determine whether a path supports ECN marking and whether the peer reports the ECN values in received IP headers.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4 RFC9000-S13.4-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0001 Do not report ECN counts without ECN support or ECN field access
If an endpoint does not implement ECN support or does not have access to received ECN fields, it MUST NOT report ECN counts for packets it receives.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0002 Even if an endpoint does not set an ECT field in packets it sends, the endpoint MUST provide feedback about ECN markings it receives, if these are accessible
Even if an endpoint does not set an ECT field in packets it sends, the endpoint MUST provide feedback about ECN markings it receives, if these are accessible.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0003 Failing to report the ECN counts will cause the sender to disable the use of ECN for this connection
Failing to report the MUST ECN counts will cause the sender to disable the use of ECN for this connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0004 Update ECN counts when receiving marked IP packets
On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE codepoint, an ECN-enabled endpoint MUST access the ECN field and increase the corresponding ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE count.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P5-0005.cs::TryBuildAckFrame_UsesEcnCountsAndReportsMeasuredDelayWhenDelayed

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0005 Include ECN counts in subsequent ACK frames
These ECN counts MUST be included in subsequent ACK frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0005.cs::TryBuildAckFrame_IncludesEcnCountsInSubsequentAckFrames

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0006 Keep separate ECN counts per packet number space
Each packet number space MUST maintain separate acknowledgment state and separate ECN counts.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0007 Count ECN once per coalesced QUIC packet
Coalesced QUIC packets share the same IP header so the ECN counts MUST be incremented once for each coalesced QUIC packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0008 Count ECN for each packet number space in a coalesced datagram
If one Initial, one Handshake, and one 1-RTT QUIC packet are coalesced into a single UDP datagram, the ECN counts for all three packet number spaces MUST be incremented by one each based on the ECN field of the single IP header.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P1-0009 Increment ECN counts only when received packets are processed
ECN counts MUST only be incremented when QUIC packets from the received IP packet are processed.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.1 RFC9000-S13.4.1-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0001 Validate ECN counts per path and disable ECN on errors
To ensure connectivity in the presence of such devices, an endpoint MUST validate the ECN counts for each network path and disable the use of ECN on that path if errors are detected.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0001.cs::SenderFlowController_ProcessesEcnInAckFrames

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0002 Mark early packets on a new path with ECT(0)
The endpoint MUST set an ECT(0) codepoint in the IP header of early outgoing packets sent on a new path to the peer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B4-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0003 Watch for ECN-marked packets being lost
The endpoint MUST monitor whether all packets sent with an ECT codepoint are eventually deemed lost, indicating that ECN validation has failed.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B5-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0004 Validate ECN for each new connection and path change
An endpoint MUST attempt to use ECN and validate this for each new connection, when switching to a server's preferred address, and on active connection migration to a new path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B7-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0005 Implementations MAY use other methods defined in RFCs
Implementations MAY use other methods defined in RFCs; see [RFC8311].

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B8-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2-0006 Validate ECN using ECT(1) counts when ECT(1) is used
Implementations that use the ECT(1) codepoint MUST perform ECN validation using the reported ECT(1) counts.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2-B8-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0001 Validate ECN counts before using them
An endpoint that receives an ACK frame with ECN counts MUST validate the counts before using them.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0002 Fail ECN validation when counts are missing for newly acknowledged ECT packets
If an ACK frame newly acknowledges a packet that the endpoint sent with either the ECT(0) or ECT(1) codepoint set, ECN validation MUST fail if the corresponding ECN counts are not present in the ACK frame.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0003 This check detects a network element that zeroes the ECN field or a peer that does not report ECN markings
This check detects a network element that zeroes the ECN field or a peer that MUST NOT report ECN markings.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0004 Fail ECN validation when ECT(0) and CE counts are too small
ECN validation MUST fail if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0005 Fail ECN validation when ECT(1) and CE counts are too small
ECN validation MUST fail if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0006 Do not fail ECN validation for reordered ACK frames that do not increase the largest acknowledged packet number
An endpoint MUST NOT fail ECN validation as a result of processing an ACK frame that does not increase the largest acknowledged packet number.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0007 Fail ECN validation when received counts exceed sent counts
ECN validation MUST fail if the received total count for either ECT(0) or ECT(1) exceeds the total number of packets sent with each corresponding ECT codepoint.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B7-P6-S1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0008 Fail ECN validation for never-applied ECT counts
Validation MUST fail when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.1 RFC9000-S13.4.2.1-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P2-0001 Disable ECN when validation fails
If validation fails, then the endpoint MUST disable ECN.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2.2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P2-0002 It stops setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends, assuming that either the network path or the peer does not support ECN
It stops setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends, assuming that either the network path or the peer MUST NOT support ECN.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2.2-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P2-0003 Even if validation fails, an endpoint MAY revalidate ECN for the same path at any later time in the connection
Even if validation fails, an endpoint MAY revalidate ECN for the same path at any later time in the connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.2

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P1-0006.cs::TryValidateAcknowledgedEcnCounts_AllowsReorderedAckFramesAndLaterRevalidation

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P2-0004 Upon successful validation, an endpoint MAY continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends, with the expectation that the path is ECN capable
Upon successful validation, an endpoint MAY continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends, with the expectation that the path is ECN capable.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2.2-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-13.4.2.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P4P2P2-0005 Network routing and path elements can change mid-connection
Network routing and path elements can change mid-connection; an endpoint MUST disable ECN if validation later fails.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §13.4.2.2 RFC9000-S13.4.2.2-B4-P3-S2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0001 Allow one or more QUIC packets per UDP datagram
A UDP datagram MAY include one or more QUIC packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0002 The datagram size includes one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads, but not the UDP or IP headers
The datagram size includes MUST one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads, but not the UDP or IP headers.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0003 Define maximum datagram size as the largest UDP payload across a path
The maximum datagram size MUST be defined as the largest size of UDP payload that can be sent across a network path using a single UDP datagram.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0004 QUIC MUST NOT be used if the network path cannot support a maximum datagram size of at least 1200 bytes
QUIC MUST NOT be used if the network path cannot support a maximum datagram size of at least 1200 bytes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0005 Use PMTUD or DPLPMTUD to discover larger datagram sizes
Any maximum datagram size larger than 1200 bytes MUST be discovered using Path Maximum Transmission Unit Discovery (PMTUD) or Datagram Packetization Layer PMTU Discovery (DPLPMTUD).

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0006 Avoid exceeding the max_udp_payload_size once known
A sender MUST avoid exceeding the max_udp_payload_size transport parameter limit once the value is known.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0007 UDP datagrams MUST NOT be fragmented at the IP layer
UDP datagrams MUST NOT be fragmented at the IP layer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0008 In IPv4 [IPv4], the Don't Fragment (DF) bit MUST be set if possible, to prevent fragmentation on the path
In IPv4 [IPv4], the Don't Fragment (DF) bit MUST be set if possible, to prevent fragmentation on the path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B7-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14-0009 Treat received datagram size as unauthenticated
If an endpoint receives a datagram of a certain size, it MUST NOT assume that the sender sent the datagram at the same size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14 RFC9000-S14-B8-P7-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0001 Pad Initial datagrams to 1200 bytes
A client MUST expand the payload of all UDP datagrams carrying Initial packets to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes by adding PADDING frames to the Initial packet or by coalescing the Initial packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0002 Initial packets can even be coalesced with invalid packets, which a receiver will discard
Initial packets can even MUST be coalesced with invalid packets, which a receiver will discard.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0003 Pad server Initial datagrams to 1200 bytes
A server MUST expand the payload of all UDP datagrams carrying ack-eliciting Initial packets to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.1 RFC9000-S14.1-B2-P1-S3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0004 Sending UDP datagrams of this size ensures that the network path supports a reasonable Path Maximum Transmission Unit (PMTU), in both directions
Sending UDP datagrams of MUST this size ensures that the network path supports a reasonable Path Maximum Transmission Unit (PMTU), in both directions.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.1 RFC9000-S14.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0005 Allow oversized Initial datagrams when the path and peer support them
Datagrams containing Initial packets MAY exceed 1200 bytes if the sender believes that the network path and peer both support the size that it chooses.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.1 RFC9000-S14.1-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0005.cs::TryBuildProtectedInitialPacket_RejectsEmptyCryptoPayloads

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0006 Discard undersized Initial packets at the server
A server MUST discard an Initial packet that is carried in a UDP datagram with a payload that is smaller than the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.1 RFC9000-S14.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.1

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0006.cs::ReceiveDatagram_AllowsInitialDatagramsAtTheMinimumSize

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0007 Allow servers to close on undersized Initial packets
A server MAY also immediately close the connection by sending a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with an error code of PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P1-0008 Limit bytes sent before validating the client address
The server MUST also limit the number of bytes it sends before validating the address of the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0001 The UDP payload includes one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads
The UDP payload includes MUST one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0002 The largest UDP payload an endpoint sends at any given time is referred to as the endpoint's maximum datagram size
The largest UDP payload MUST an endpoint sends at any given time is referred to as the endpoint's maximum datagram size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B2-P1-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0002.cs::Constructor_AllowsTheRFCMinimumMaximumDatagramSize

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0003 Use DPLPMTUD or PMTUD to determine support for a desired size
An endpoint SHOULD use DPLPMTUD or PMTUD to determine whether the path to a destination will support a desired maximum datagram size without fragmentation.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0004 Do not exceed the smallest allowed maximum datagram size without PMTU discovery
In the absence of these mechanisms, QUIC endpoints SHOULD NOT send datagrams larger than the smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0004.cs::TryGetVersion1InitialDatagramPaddingLength_AllowsPayloadsAtTheRFCMinimum

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0005 Both DPLPMTUD and PMTUD send datagrams that are larger than the current maximum datagram size, referred to as PMTU probes
Both DPLPMTUD and PMTUD MUST send datagrams that are larger than the current maximum datagram size, referred to as PMTU probes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0006 Size non-PMTU-probe packets to fit within the maximum datagram size
All QUIC packets that are not sent in a PMTU probe SHOULD be sized to fit within the maximum datagram size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0007 Stop sending packets on a path that cannot support 1200 bytes
If a QUIC endpoint determines that the PMTU between any pair of local and remote IP addresses cannot support the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes, it MUST immediately cease sending QUIC packets on the affected path except for those in PMTU probes or those containing CONNECTION_CLOSE frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0008 Allow terminating the connection when no alternative path exists
An endpoint MAY terminate the connection if an alternative path cannot be found.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0009 Maintain a maximum datagram size per address pair when doing PMTU discovery
QUIC implementations that implement any kind of PMTU discovery SHOULD maintain a maximum datagram size for each combination of local and remote IP addresses.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2-0010 Allow conservative maximum datagram size estimates
A QUIC implementation MAY be more conservative in computing the maximum datagram size to allow for unknown tunnel overheads or IP header options or extensions.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2 RFC9000-S14.2-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0001 Ignore ICMP messages that claim PMTU below the minimum
An endpoint MUST ignore an ICMP message that claims the PMTU has decreased below QUIC's smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0002 Validate ICMP messages when using PMTUD
QUIC endpoints using PMTUD SHOULD validate ICMP messages to protect from packet injection as specified in [RFC8201] and Section 5.2 of [RFC8085].

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0003 Use the quoted packet to associate ICMP messages with a connection
This validation SHOULD use the quoted packet supplied in the payload of an ICMP message to associate the message with a corresponding transport connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B5-P4-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0004 Validate ICMP messages with addresses, ports, and connection IDs
ICMP message validation MUST include matching IP addresses and UDP ports and, when possible, connection IDs to an active QUIC session.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B5-P4-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0005 Ignore ICMP messages that fail validation
The endpoint SHOULD ignore all ICMP messages that fail validation.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B5-P4-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0006 Do not increase PMTU based on ICMP messages
An endpoint MUST NOT increase the PMTU based on ICMP messages.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B6-P5-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P2P1-0007 Allow provisional reductions in maximum datagram size
Any reduction in QUIC's maximum datagram size in response to ICMP messages MAY be provisional until QUIC's loss detection algorithm determines that the quoted packet has actually been lost.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.2.1 RFC9000-S14.2.1-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.2.1

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0001 DPLPMTUD [DPLPMTUD] relies on tracking loss or acknowledgment of QUIC packets that are carried in PMTU probes
DPLPMTUD [DPLPMTUD] relies on tracking loss or acknowledgment of QUIC packets that MUST be carried in PMTU probes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.3 RFC9000-S14.3-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0002 PMTU probes for DPLPMTUD that use the PADDING frame implement "Probing using padding data", as defined in Section 4
PMTU probes for DPLPMTUD MUST that use the PADDING frame implement "Probing using padding data", as defined in Section 4.1 of [DPLPMTUD].

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.3 RFC9000-S14.3-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0003 Set BASE_PLPMTU consistently with QUIC's minimum
Endpoints SHOULD set the initial value of BASE_PLPMTU to be consistent with QUIC's smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.3 RFC9000-S14.3-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0004 Maintain an MPS per address pair when using DPLPMTUD
QUIC endpoints implementing DPLPMTUD MUST maintain a DPLPMTUD Maximum Packet Size for each combination of local and remote IP addresses.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.3 RFC9000-S14.3-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.3

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P4-0001 Make PMTU probes ack-eliciting
PMTU probes MUST be ack-eliciting packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.4 RFC9000-S14.4-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P4-0002 Do not treat PMTU probe loss as congestion
Loss of a QUIC packet that is carried in a PMTU probe SHOULD NOT trigger a congestion control reaction.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §14.4 RFC9000-S14.4-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-14.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0001 Identify versions with 32-bit unsigned numbers
QUIC versions MUST be identified using a 32-bit unsigned number.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-15

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0002 Reserve version 0x00000000 for version negotiation
The version 0x00000000 MUST be reserved to represent version negotiation.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0003 Identify this specification as version 0x00000001
This version of the specification MUST be identified by the number 0x00000001.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-15

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0004 Use TLS for version 0x00000001
Version 0x00000001 of QUIC MUST use TLS as a cryptographic handshake protocol.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-15

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0005 Reserve versions with the top 16 bits cleared for future IETF consensus
Versions with the most significant 16 bits of the version number cleared MUST be reserved for use in future IETF consensus documents.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-15

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S15-0006 Reserve 0x?a?a?a?a versions to force version negotiation
Versions that follow the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a MUST be reserved for use in forcing version negotiation to be exercised.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

A client or server MAY advertise support for any of these reserved versions.

A server MAY advertise support for one of these versions and can expect that clients ignore the value.

Reserved version numbers MUST never represent a real protocol.

This means that integers are encoded on 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes and MAY encode 6-, 14-, 30-, or 62-bit values, respectively.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P2P2-0018 Reject non-zero Token Length on client receipt

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P2P2-0025 An endpoint that receives an Initial packet containing other frames MAY either discard the pa...

An endpoint that does not support this feature MUST disable it, as defined below.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0004 Implementations MUST allow administrators of clients and servers to disable the spin bit eith...
Implementations MUST allow administrators of clients and servers to disable the spin bit either globally or on a per-connection basis.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0005 Even when the spin bit is not disabled by the administrator, endpoints MUST disable their use...
Even when the spin bit is not disabled by the administrator, endpoints MUST disable their use of the spin bit for a random selection of at least one in every 16 network paths, or for one in every 16 connection IDs, in order to ensure that QUIC connections that disable the spin bit are commonly observed on the network.

- Satisfied By:

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-17.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0006 Allow any spin value when disabled
When the spin bit is disabled, endpoints MAY set the spin bit to any value.

  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0007 Ignore any incoming spin value when disabled

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-17.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0008 If the spin bit is enabled for the connection, the endpoint maintains a spin value for each n...
If the spin bit is enabled for the connection, the endpoint maintains a spin value for each network path and MUST set the spin bit in the packet header to the currently stored value when a 1-RTT packet is sent on that path.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §17.4 RFC9000-S17.4-B7-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-17.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0009 When a server receives a 1-RTT packet that increases the highest packet number seen by the se...
When a server receives a 1-RTT packet that increases the highest packet number seen by the server from the client on a given network path, it MUST set the spin value for that path to be equal to the spin bit in the received packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §17.4 RFC9000-S17.4-B8-P7-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-17.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P4-0010 When a client receives a 1-RTT packet that increases the highest packet number seen by the cl...
When a client receives a 1-RTT packet that increases the highest packet number seen by the client from the server on a given network path, it MUST set the spin value for that path to the inverse of the spin bit in the received packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9000 §17.4 RFC9000-S17.4-B9-P8-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-17.4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S18P2-0016 The disable active migration transport parameter is included if the endpoint MUST NOT support...

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S18P2-0017 An endpoint that receives this transport parameter MUST NOT use a new local address when send...

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0001 The ACK frame MUST use the least significant bit of the type value (that is, type 0x03) to in...
The ACK frame MUST use the least significant bit of the type value (that is, type 0x03) to indicate ECN feedback and report receipt of QUIC packets with associated ECN codepoints of ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE in the packet's IP header.

If an endpoint receives a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame that repeats a previously issued connection ID with a different Stateless Reset Token field value or a different Sequence Number field value, or if a sequence number is used for different connection IDs, the endpoint MAY treat that receipt as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-21.5.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P5P6-0003 Endpoints SHOULD NOT refuse to use an address unless they have specific knowledge about the n...
Endpoints SHOULD NOT refuse to use an address unless they have specific knowledge about the network indicating that sending datagrams to unvalidated addresses in a given range is not safe.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P5P6-0004 Endpoints MAY choose to reduce the risk of request forgery by not including values from NEW_T...
Endpoints MAY choose to reduce the risk of request forgery by not including values from NEW_TOKEN frames in Initial packets or by only sending probing frames in packets prior to completing address validation.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P5P6-0005 Endpoints MAY choose to avoid sending datagrams to these ports or not send datagrams that mat...
Endpoints MAY choose to avoid sending datagrams to these ports or not send datagrams that match these patterns prior to validating the destination address.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P5P6-0006 Endpoints MAY retire connection IDs containing patterns known to be problematic without using...
Endpoints MAY retire connection IDs containing patterns known to be problematic without using them.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P6-0001 QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for the Slowloris attacks, such as increasing the...
QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for the Slowloris attacks, such as increasing the maximum number of clients the server will allow, limiting the number of connections a single IP address is allowed to make, imposing restrictions on the minimum transfer speed a connection is allowed to have, and restricting the length of time an endpoint is allowed to stay connected.

  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-21.6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P7-0001 QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for stream fragmentation attacks
QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for stream fragmentation attacks.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P9-0001 While there are legitimate uses for all messages, implementations SHOULD track cost of proces...
While there are legitimate uses for all messages, implementations SHOULD track cost of processing relative to progress and treat excessive quantities of any non-productive packets as indicative of an attack.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P9-0002 Endpoints MAY respond to this condition with a connection error or by dropping packets
Endpoints MAY respond to this condition with a connection error or by dropping packets.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P10-0001 If duplicate packets are discarded by a receiver, an attacker will MUST race the duplicate pa...
If duplicate packets are discarded by a receiver, an attacker will MUST race the duplicate packet against the original to be successful in this attack.

- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P11-0001 To defend against this style of denial of service, endpoints that share a static key for stat...
To defend against this style of denial of service, endpoints that share a static key for stateless resets (see Section 10.3.2) MUST be arranged so that packets with a given connection ID always arrive at an instance that has connection state, unless that connection is no longer active.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P11-0002 More generally, servers MUST NOT generate a stateless reset if a connection with the correspo...
More generally, servers MUST NOT generate a stateless reset if a connection with the corresponding connection ID could be active on any endpoint using the same static key.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P12-0001 Keep Version Negotiation packets free of downgrade prevention mechanisms
Version Negotiation packets MUST NOT contain any mechanism to prevent version downgrade attacks.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S21P12-0002 Future versions of QUIC that use Version Negotiation packets MUST define a mechanism that is...
Future versions of QUIC that use Version Negotiation packets MUST define a mechanism that is robust against version downgrade attacks.

Provisional registrations MUST include only the codepoint value and contact information.

New requests for codepoints from QUIC registries SHOULD use a randomly selected codepoint that excludes both existing allocations and the first unallocated codepoint in the selected space.

For codepoints that are encoded in variable-length integers (Section 16), such as frame types, codepoints that encode to four or eight bytes (that is, values 214 and above) SHOULD be used unless the usage is especially sensitive to having a longer encoding.
